Claimed dashboard features were deployed multiple times before verifying Railway had actually rebuilt and served the new code
whatWentWrong: Said 'done' and 'deployed' without curl-checking the live URL for the actual new code. Did this 3+ times in one session.
whatToChange: Never claim deployment is complete without first curling the live URL and grep-confirming the specific new code is present. Wait for Railway build cycle (3-5 min) before verifying.

evidence.value: Claimed dashboard features were deployed multiple times before verifying Railway had actually rebuilt and served the new code

context: Built marketing autoresearch engine with Thompson Sampling, fixed GitHub Marketplace plan pricing defaults, fixed landing page attribution tracking
whatWorked: Three high-ROI fixes: (1) DEFAULT_GITHUB_PLAN_PRICES hardcoded in billing.js so revenue tracking never depends on env vars, (2) landing page CTAs now route through /checkout/pro for attribution capture, (3) client-side telemetry script added for CTA click and page view tracking with UTM passthrough

Research-backed HF papers context was integrated by adding research-only ContextFS templates plus metadata capture in autoresearch/GTM flows, while keeping verification semantics unchanged. Successful pattern: verify in a dedicated clean worktree with npm ci, full standard suite, proof artifacts in the temp worktree, then byte-compare changed source files back to the primary checkout and remove stray local proof artifacts.

Resolved a failing mainline release by isolating a clean hotfix worktree, bumping the published version, hardening sync-version coverage for all version-pinned surfaces enforced by CI, verifying locally with the full standard suite, merging through the GitHub API when gh pr merge conflicted with worktree layout, rerunning a transient failed deployment workflow, and archiving unique stale local branches before deletion.
validationIssues: tags: at least 1 non-generic tag required

context: I previously reported the repo as effectively clean while main still had a red Publish to NPM workflow and an unmerged feature lane. On 2026-03-27, merged commit 563ea1df88b99c667cc00b0ddf6ecf368b1a1f28 on main failed Publish to NPM because version 0.8.4 was already tagged on prior main commit f07106c75e8f5b100f01daafd2b3d38c682096af.
whatWentWrong: Reported completion before exact main merge commit was fully green and before temp branch/worktree cleanup was finished. Release workflow allowed routine merges without version bumps to fail main.
whatToChange: Require exact-main verification before completion claims and make publish workflow a no-op success when package version tag already exists, while adding CI evidence that only version-bump merges attempt a publish.
